reasoning: Dracunculiasis (Guinea worm disease) currently has no vaccine. Furthermore, there is no active research aiming to develop one because the disease is on the verge of global eradication through water filtration, vector control, and behavioral interventions. The Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases" correctly leaves the vaccine column blank for this disease, which the resolution criteria interpret as "No". Given the CDC and WHO's recent updates (as of late 2025/early 2026) unequivocally confirming the absence of a vaccine, and the extremely short timeline (1 to 4 weeks), it is practically impossible that a vaccine will be developed and added to the table. The only conceivable path to a "Yes" resolution is transient Wikipedia vandalism or an erroneous edit that happens to survive on the resolution dates. Thus, the probabilities are set at the system floor of 0.05 for the closer date and 0.06 for the later date (satisfying the rule that uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon).
